cached_output:
AI at Work — Daily Digest
Generated 2026-03-25 13:00 UTC
==================================================

1. AI note taking startup Granola raised $125M led by Index Ventures at a $1.5B valuation, and plans Claude integrations and agentic AI features in the next year (Rebecca Torrence/Bloomberg)
   http://www.techmeme.com/260325/p15#a260325p15
   → Professionals can now invest in Granola for more efficient note-taking, with plans to integrate Claude.

2. SpecEyes: Accelerating Agentic Multimodal LLMs via Speculative Perception and Planning
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23483v1
   → SpecEyes aims to enhance multimodal LLMs by improving their visual reasoning capabilities through speculative perception and planning.

3. ReqFusion: A Multi-Provider Framework for Automated PEGS Analysis Across Software Domains
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23482v1
   → ReqFusion automates requirements engineering tasks, streamlining the software development process for professionals.

4. Designing Agentic AI-Based Screening for Portfolio Investment
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23300v1
   → The new agentic AI platform offers a novel approach to portfolio investment screening, focusing on specialized agents with specific responsibilities.

5. Curriculum-Driven 3D CT Report Generation via Language-Free Visual Grafting and Zone-Constrained Compression
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23308v1
   → This innovative method addresses challenges in automated radiology report generation from CT volumes using LLMs and visual grafting techniques.

6. Chinese AI company Kuaishou reports Q4 revenue up 12% YoY to ~$5.7B, meeting est., adjusted net income up 16% YoY, and Kling text-to-video revenue of ~$49M (Rachel Yeo/Bloomberg)
   http://www.techmeme.com/260325/p11#a260325p11
   → Kuaishou's robust financial performance validates its AI-driven strategies, particularly in text-to-video conversion for increased revenue growth.

7. Evaluating LLM-Based Test Generation Under Software Evolution
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23443v1
   → Evaluating LLM-based test generation helps ensure that tests accurately reflect program behavior rather than merely reproducing existing ones.

8. Beyond Preset Identities: How Agents Form Stances and Boundaries in Generative Societies
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23406v1
   → Understanding how agents form stances and boundaries is crucial for improving the social behaviors of large language models in generative societies.

9. Show HN: Loom: A Compiler for Agentic Workflows (Go, Python, Rust)
   https://github.com/acartine/loom
   → Loom provides a compiler for agentic workflows, offering developers flexibility across multiple programming languages like Go, Python, and Rust.

10. Code Review Agent Benchmark
   https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23448v1
   → The Code Review Agent Benchmark highlights the need to ensure AI-generated code quality as more agents enter into code writing roles.
